title: I am setting up an Obsidian vault as a local AI me

children.text: I am setting up an Obsidian vault as a local AI memory layer. Generate a folder structure separating 'Church and State'. I need a 'Human' folder for my personal notes, and an 'AI' folder for agent skills, workflows, templates, and SOPs. Output the structure clearly so I can build it.

promptContent.text: I am setting up an Obsidian vault as a local AI memory layer. Generate a folder structure separating 'Church and State'. I need a 'Human' folder for my personal notes, and an 'AI' folder for agent skills, workflows, templates, and SOPs. Output the structure clearly so I can build it.

messages.content:
<smtcmp_block language="markdown">
```
Welcome.md

## 📁 Folder Structure

```
├── 🕍 Human/
│   ├── Daily Notes/
│   ├── Journal/
│   ├── People/
│   ├── Projects/
│   └── Resources/
│
├– 🤖 AI/
│   ├── Skills/
│   ├── Workflows/
│   ├── Templates/
│   └── SOPs/
```

## 📝 Quick Reference

- **Human/** = Personal notes, journal, research, projects
- **AI/** = Agent capabilities, automation, system prompts, standard operating procedures
</smtcmp_block>

children.text: write a python code that can be run in cmd to achieve this.

promptContent.text: write a python code that can be run in cmd to achieve this.

messages.content:
<smtcmp_block language="markdown">
## Python Script for Folder Structure

```python
# Create Obsidian AI Memory Vault Structure
import os

BASE_NAME = "my-ai-memory-vault"
HUMAN_DIR = "Human"
AI_DIR = "AI"

# Human folders
HUMAN_SUBDIRS = ["Daily Notes", "Journal", "People", "Projects", "Resources"]

# AI folders
AI_SUBDIRS = ["Skills", "Workflows", "Templates", "SOPs"]

# Create Human directories
os.makedirs(BASE_NAME, exist_ok=True)
for sub in HUMAN_SUBDIRS:
    os.makedirs(f"{BASE_NAME}/{HUMAN_DIR}/{sub}", exist_ok=True)

# Create AI directories
for sub in AI_SUBDIRS:
    os.makedirs(f"{BASE_NAME}/{AI_DIR}/{sub}", exist_ok=True)

print("✅ Vault structure created successfully!")
print(f"📁 Root: {BASE_NAME}")
```

## Usage

Run in cmd:
```cmd
python generate_structure.py
```
</smtcmp_block>

children.text: no I want to run directly in cmd

promptContent.text: no I want to run directly in cmd

messages.content:
<smtcmp_block language="markdown">
## Batch File for Folder Structure (Windows cmd)

Save as `create_vault.bat`:

```batch
@echo off

set BASE_NAME=my-ai-memory-vault
set HUMAN_DIR=Human
set AI_DIR=AI

set HUMAN_SUBDIRS=Daily Notes Journal People Projects Resources
set AI_SUBDIRS=Skills Workflows Templates SOPs

set /a COUNT=0
set HUMAN_COUNT=0
set AI_COUNT=0

if not exist "%BASE_NAME%" mkdir "%BASE_NAME%" else echo %BASE_NAME% exists

for %%H in (%HUMAN_SUBDIRS%) do (
    mkdir "%BASE_NAME%\%HUMAN_DIR%\%%H"
    set /a HUMAN_COUNT+=1
    echo Created %HUMAN_DIR%\%%H%
)

for %%A in (%AI_SUBDIRS%) do (
    mkdir "%BASE_NAME%\%AI_DIR%\%%A"
    set /a AI_COUNT+=1
    echo Created %AI_DIR%\%%A%
)

echo %DATE% %TIME% - Vault created successfully!
echo Created %HUMAN_COUNT% Human folders and %AI_COUNT% AI folders
pause
```

## Usage

```cmd
create_vault.bat
```

Or run manually in cmd:

<file content="mkdir my-ai-memory-vault\Human\\Daily Notes my-ai-memory-vault\Human\\Journal my-ai-memory-vault\Human\\People my-ai-memory-vault\Human\\Projects my-ai-memory-vault\Human\\Resources my-ai-memory-vault\AI\\Skills my-ai-memory-vault\AI\\Workflows my-ai-memory-vault\AI\\Templates my-ai-memory-vault\AI\\SOPs" filename="cmd_create_vault.cmd" language="markdown">
</file>
</smtcmp_block>

messages.content:
<smtcmp_block language="markdown">
## Run Directly (No Intermediate File)

Copy-paste this directly into **cmd.exe**:

```cmd
@echo off
mkdir my-ai-memory-vault\Human\DailyNotes
mkdir my-ai-memory-vault\Human\Journal
mkdir my-ai-memory-vault\Human\People
mkdir my-ai-memory-vault\Human\Projects
mkdir my-ai-memory-vault\Human\Resources
mkdir my-ai-memory-vault\AI\Skills
mkdir my-ai-memory-vault\AI\Workflows
mkdir my-ai-memory-vault\AI\Templates
mkdir my-ai-memory-vault\AI\SOPs
echo ✅ Vault folder structure created!
```

## Usage

1. Open **Command Prompt** (cmd.exe)
2. Navigate to your vault location (or any folder)
3. Paste and press **ENTER**

No file creation needed!
</smtcmp_block>

<smtcmp_block language="markdown">
## Run Directly (No File, No Prefix)

Copy-paste this into **cmd.exe**:

```cmd
mkdir Human
mkdir Human\\DailyNotes
mkdir Human\\Journal
mkdir Human\\People
mkdir Human\\Projects
mkdir Human\\Resources
mkdir AI
mkdir AI\\Skills
mkdir AI\\Workflows
mkdir AI\\Templates
mkdir AI\\SOPs
echo ✅ Complete!
```

## Usage

1. Open **Command Prompt** (cmd.exe)
2. Navigate to where you want the vault (or use `.`):
   ```cmd
   mkdir ..\\AI_Storage
   ```
3. Run the mkdir commands above
4. Paste and press **ENTER**

Folders created directly in current directory with **AI/** and **Human/** roots!
</smtcmp_block>

promptContent.text: can you generate a JIRA board like tracking system for me

messages.content:
<smtcmp_block filename="Project Tracking System.md" language="markdown">
```
# 🏗️ Project Tracking System (Jira-like for Obsidian)

---

## 📂 Folder Structure

```
Projects/
├── Epic Title.md
├── Projects/{project-name}/
│   ├── Backlog.md
│   ├── Active/
│   │   ├── In Progress/
│   │   └── Review/
│   ├── Done/
│   └── {task-id}.md
│
└── Boards/
    ├── Sprint 1.md
    └── Sprint 2.md
```

---

## 🧭 Epic Template

```markdown
# {{project-name}}

---
date: {{today}}
tags: [project/{{name}}]

## 📦 Project Overview

- **Objective:** 
- **Duration:** 
- **Priority:** 

---

## 📋 Tasks

| ID | Title | Status | Owner | Due Date | Notes |
|----|-------|-----|------|----------|-|
|---|---|-|--|---|---|

---

## 📊 Progress Overview

- Completed: / 
- In Progress: 
- In Backlog: 

---

## 📝 Notes

*
---
```

---

## 🟢 Active Task Template

```markdown
# {{task-id}}: {{title}}

---
date: {{date}}
tags: [task/{{task-id}}]
aliases: [alias]

## 📌 Summary

What needs to be done:

## 🔗 Related

- [[Epic Name]]
- [[Related Tasks]]

## 📝 Progress Notes

1. 
2.

messages.content:
<smtcmp_block filename="Kanban Project System.md" language="markdown">
# 🏗️ Kanban Board (Jira-like with Drag-Drop)

---

## 📂 Recommended Setup

### Required Plugins (Community)
1. **Advanced Kanban** - Drag-drop Kanban boards
2. **Calendar** - Timeline view
3. **Templater** - Auto-date fields

### Folder Structure

```
Projects/
├── Sprint 1 Kanban.md
├── Backlog.md
└── Done.md

Projects/
└── {Project-Name}/
    ├── Epics.md
    ├── Tasks.md
    └── Sub-tasks/
```

---

## 🟣 Kanban Board Layout

```markdown
# 📊 {{Project-Name}} - Kanban Board

---
date: {{date}}
tags: [project/board/{{name}}]

---

## 🏗️ Board Columns

- **📥 Backlog** - Ideas, research
- **📋 To Do** - Ready for work
- **🔄 In Progress** - Currently working
- **🧩 Review** - QA, stakeholder review
- **✅ Done** - Completed

---

## 💡 How to Use

1. Install **Advanced Kanban plugin**
2. Create columns in board view
3. Drag tasks between states
4. Click tasks to open in editor
5. Use `Templater` for quick cards

---

## 📝 Task Card Template

```markdown
---
date: {{date}}
status: todo
priority: medium
epic: 
---

# {{Title}}

---

## 🎯 Objective

- 

## 📚 Details

- 
- 

## 🔗 Related

- [[Epic]]
- [[Related Tasks]]

## ✅ Checklist

- [ ] 
- [ ] 

## 🧭 Next Steps

--
```

---

## 📊 Dashboard View (Kanban Table)

```markdown
# 🎯 Project Dashboard

---
tags: [project/{{project-name}}]

## 📁 Board Summary

| Column | Count |
|---|-|
| Backlog | |
| To Do | |
| In Progress | |
| Review | |
| Done | |

## 🎯 Top Priority Items

- 
- 

## ⚠️ Blockers

- 
- 
```

---
</smtcmp_block>

## 🔧 Setup Instructions

1. **Install Plugins**: Settings → Community Plugins
2. Enable: `Advanced Kanban`, `Templater`, `Calendar`
3. Create board using plugin's board view
4. Configure columns per Jira workflow
5. Drag tasks in board view
6. Click for full editor view

## 📌 Pro Tip

- Use `Templater`'s `{{query}}` to auto-fill status
- Tag cards by priority: [priority/high], [priority/medium]
- Sync with Obsidian's graph for epics
- Add date tags for timeline visualization
Yes, I am here. How can I help you further with your Obsidian setup?

children.text: Help me to design a JIRA board like task tracker. Feel free to use plugin

promptContent.text: Help me to design a JIRA board like task tracker. Feel free to use plugin

messages.content:
To build a true JIRA-like experience in Obsidian with drag-and-drop, you need a "Task-as-a-Note" architecture. This allows each task to have its own metadata, attachments, and deep documentation.

### 🛠️ Required Plugins
1. **Kanban**: For the drag-and-drop board view.
2. **Dataview**: To create "Jira Dashboards" (automated tables/summaries).
3. **Templater**: To generate new tasks with standardized metadata.
4. **Metadata Menu** (Optional): To add dropdown menus for "Status" or "Priority" inside notes.

---

### 📂 Recommended Folder Structure
```text
Projects/
├── 📋 Boards/          (Kanban Board files)
├── 🚀 Sprints/         (Sprint planning notes)
├── 🎫 Tasks/           (Individual task notes - 1 note per task)
└── 🏢 Epics/           (High-level project notes)
```

---

### 📄 1. The "Issue" Template (The Task)
Every task starts from this template. It uses YAML properties to store JIRA-like data.

<smtcmp_block filename="Templates/New Task Template.md" language="markdown">
```markdown
---
type: task
status: todo
priority: medium
assignee: Me
sprint: Sprint 1
due_date: <% tp.date.now("YYYY-MM-DD") %>
epic: 
tags: [task]
---

# 🎫 {{title}}

## 📝 Description
<!-- Describe the requirements here -->

## 🎯 Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 
- [ ] 

## 🔗 Links & References
- **Epic:** [[ ]]
- **Related:** 

## 🪵 Activity Log
- **<% tp.date.now("YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm") %>**: Task Created.
```
</smtcmp_block>

---

### 📋 2. The Kanban Board (The "Sprint Board")
Configure the **Kanban Plugin** to use these columns. Instead of writing text, you will drag **links to your task notes** between columns.

<smtcmp_block filename="Boards/Sprint 1 Board.md" language="markdown">
```markdown
---
kanban-plugin: basic
---

## 📥 Backlog
- [[Task-123|Refactor API]]
- [[Task-124|Update Documentation]]

## 📋 To Do
- [[Task-125|Fix Login Bug]]

## 🔄 In Progress
- [[Task-126|Database Migration]]

## ✅ Done
- [[Task-127|Setup Project Structure]]
```
</smtcmp_block>

---

### 📊 3. The Project Dashboard (The "JIRA Dashboard")
Use **Dataview** to create an automated overview of all active tasks across all boards.

<smtcmp_block filename="Boards/Project Dashboard.md" language:="markdown">
```markdown
# 📊 Project Dashboard

## ⚠️ High Priority Blockers
```dataview
TABLE priority, due_date, assignee
FROM "Tasks"
WHERE priority = "high" AND status != "done"
SORT due_date ASC
```

## 📅 Upcoming Deadlines
```dataview
TABLE status, sprint
FROM "Tasks"
WHERE due_date >= date(today)
SORT due_date ASC
LIMIT 10
```

## 📈 Sprint Progress
```dataview
LIST
FROM "Tasks"
WHERE status = "in-progress"
```
```
</smtcmp_block>

---

### 🚀 Workflow Summary
1. **Create a Task**: Use `Templater` to create a new note in the `Tasks/` folder.
2. **Assign to Board**: Open your **Kanban Board**, and drag the link to your new task into the "To Do" column.
3. **Update Status**: As you work, drag the task link in the Kanban board from `In Progress` $\rightarrow$ `Done`.
4. **Monitor**: Check the **Dashboard** to see automated reports on blockers and deadlines.

children.text: I just want boards and tasks not epics and sprints

promptContent.text: I just want boards and tasks not epics and sprints

messages.content:
To keep it strictly to **Boards** and **Tasks**, we will use the **Kanban** plugin for the visual board and **Dataview** for a simple task list view.

### 📂 Simplified Folder Structure
```text
Tasks/          (All individual task notes)
Boards/          (Your Kanban board files)
```

---

### 📄 1. The Task Template
Use this to create every new task. It contains the data needed for the board and lists.

<smtcmp_block filename="Templates/Task Template.md" language="markdown">
```markdown
---
status: todo
priority: medium
due_date: {{date}}
tags: [task]
---

# 📝 Task: {{title}}

## 🎯 Description
- 

## ✅ Checklist
- [ ] 
```
</smtcmp_block>

---

### 📋 2. The Kanban Board
Use the **Kanban Plugin**. Create columns for your workflow. You will drag **links to your task notes** between these columns.

<smtcmp_block filename="Boards/Main Board.md" language="markdown">
```markdown
---
kanban-plugin: basic
---

## 📥 Backlog
- [[Task 1]]
- [[Task 2]]

## 📋 To Do
- [[Task 3]]

## 🔄 In Progress
- [[Task 4]]

## ✅ Done
- [[Task 5]]
```
</smtcmp_block>

---

### 📊 3. The Task List (Automatic View)
Use the **Dataview Plugin** to see a "Jira-style" list of all tasks, sorted by priority or due date, without opening the board.

<smtcmp_block filename="Boards/Task Overview.md" language="markdown">
```markdown
# 🗒️ All Tasks Overview

```dataview
TABLE status, priority, due_date
FROM "Tasks"
SORT priority DESC, due_date ASC
```
```
</smtcmp_block>

---

### 🚀 Execution Workflow
1.  **Create Note**: Create a new note in the `Tasks/` folder using the **Task Template**.
2.  **Add to Board**: Open `Main Board.md` and drag the link `[[Task Name]]` into the desired column.
3.  **Move Tasks**: Drag the link from `To Do` $\rightarrow$ `In Progress` $\rightarrow$ `Done` inside the Kanban view.
4.  **Monitor**: Use the `Task Overview` file to see a master list of everything you have pending.

children.text: Please give instruction in details like when to create which file.

promptContent.text: Please give instruction in details like when to create which file.

messages.content:
This guide follows a chronological setup. Follow these steps in order.

### Phase 1: One-Time Setup (The Foundation)

#### 1. Install Required Plugins
Go to **Settings** $\rightarrow$ **Community Plugins** $\rightarrow$ **Browse** and install:
*   **Kanban**: For the drag-and-drop interface.
*   **Dataview**: To generate the automatic task list.
*   **Templater**: To automate the creation of new tasks.

#### 2. Create Your Folder Structure
Create these three folders at the root of your vault:
*   `Tasks/` (Where all individual task notes will live)
*     `Boards/` (Where your Kanban boards and Overview will live)
*   `Templates/` (Where your Task Template will live)

#### 3. Create the Task Template
**File to create:** `Templates/Task Template.md`
Copy the following code into this file. This ensures every task has the same properties for the board to read.

<smtcmp_block filename="Templates/Task Template.md" language="markdown">
```markdown
---
status: todo
priority: medium
due_date: {{date}}
tags: [task]
---

# 📝 Task: {{title}}

## 🎯 Description
- 

## ✅ Checklist
- [ ] 
```
</smtcmp_block>

#### 4. Create the Kanban Board
**File to create:** `Boards/Main Board.md`
You can create this file manually, or right-click in your `Boards/` folder and select "New Kanban Board" if using the plugin.

<smtcmp_block filename="Boards/Main Board.md" language="markdown">
```markdown
---
kanban-plugin: basic
---

## 📥 Backlog
- 

## 📋 To Do
- 

## 🔄 In Progress
- 

## ✅ Done
- 
```
</smtcmp_block>

#### 5. Create the Task Overview (The Report)
**File to create:** `Boards/Task Overview.md`
This file is a "Read-Only" view. You never edit this file; it automatically pulls data from your `Tasks/` folder.

<smtcmp_block filename="Boards/Task Overview.md" language="markdown">
```markdown
# 🗒️ All Tasks Overview

```dataview
TABLE status, priority, due_date
FROM "Tasks"
SORT priority DESC, due_date ASC
```
```
</smtcmp_block>

---

### Phase 2: Daily Workflow (The Execution)

Now that the system is built, follow this loop every day:

**Step 1: Create a New Task**
1. Create a new note in the `Tasks/` folder.
2. Use **Templater** to apply the `Task Template`.
3. Give the note a clear title (e.g., `Fix Login Bug`).

**Step 2: Add to the Board**
1. Open `Boards/Main Board.md`.
2. In the **Backlog** or **To Do** column, type `[[` and select the name of the task you just created.
    * *Example:* `- [[Fix Login Bug]]`

**Step 3: The "Drag-and-Drop"**
1. As you start working, click and drag the `[[Fix Login Bug]]` link from **To Do** $\rightarrow$ **In Progress**.
2. When finished, drag it $\rightarrow$ **Done**.

**Step 4: Review**
1. Open `Boards/Task Overview.md` anytime to see a high-level list of everything in your `Tasks/` folder without clicking through individual files.

children.text: Can we make our boards beaultiful, basic view is not good

promptContent.text: Can we make our boards beaultiful, basic view is not good

children.text: is this task created in correct format?

promptContent.text: is this task created in correct format?

messages.content:
If you are referring to the **`Fix Login Bug.md`** file you provided at the start of our chat, it is **almost** correct, but it has three small deviations from the "Standard Template" we just built. 

To ensure your **Dataview Dashboard** and **Kanban Board** work perfectly, you should fix these:

### ❌ Issues Found
1.  **Extra Braces**: The `{{ }}` around the date and title are unnecessary and can cause formatting issues in text.
2.  **Case Sensitivity**: You used `status: ToDo`. Dataview is case-sensitive; if your dashboard looks for `todo` (lowercase), it will skip this task.
3.  **Heading Mismatch**: You used `## 🎯 Need to fix login bug`. To keep your notes consistent and easy to scan, use the standard `## 🎯 Description`.

### ✅ The Corrected Version

<smtcmp_block filename="Human/Projects/Tasks/Fix Login Bug.md" language="markdown">
```markdown
---
status: todo
priority: medium
due_date: 2014-04-22
tags: [task]
---

# 📝 Task: Fix Login Bug

## 🎯 Description
- The login button is not responding on mobile devices.

## ✅ Checklist
- [ ] Test on iOS
- [ ] Test on Android
```
</smtcmp_block>

### 💡 Pro-Tip for the future
When creating a task, **never write it manually**. Always use your **Templater** shortcut to trigger the `Task Template`. This ensures the `status`, `tags`, and `headings` are always 100% perfect.

If your tasks are not appearing, it is likely due to one of these three reasons. Please check them in this order:

### 1. Check the Folder Location
The **Task Overview** (Dataview) is specifically looking inside the `Tasks/` folder.
* **Check:** Is your task note located inside the `Tasks/` folder? 
* **Fix:** Move the task note to `Tasks/`.

### 2. Check the Metadata (YAML)
The **Task Overview** only shows notes that have the `task` tag.
* **Check:** Open your task note. Does the top section look exactly like this?
<smtcmp_block filename="Tasks/YourTask.md" language="markdown">
```markdown
---
status: todo
priority: medium
due_date: 2023-10-27
tags: [task]
---
```
* **Fix:** Ensure `tags: [task]` is present and there are no typos in the word `tags`.

### 3. Check the Kanban Board Setup
The **Kanban Board** does **not** find tasks automatically. You must manually "register" them on the board.
* **Check:** Open `Boards/Main Board.md`. Do you see the link to your task there?
* **Fix:** You must manually type the link into a column.
    * *Example:* In the **Backlog** column, type: `- [[Your Task Name]]`

---

### 🔍 Quick Debugging Checklist
| If this view is empty... | Check this first... |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Task Overview (Dataview)** | Does the note have `tags: [task]`? |
| **Task Overview (Dataview)** | Is the note inside the `Tasks/` folder? |
| **Kanban Board** | Did you manually add `[[Link to Task]]` to a column? |
| **Kanban Board** | Is the Kanban plugin enabled? |
